win_fast: Ecosystem Health reached {health}% by Day {days} — early stressor control compounded faster than the models predicted; the meadow is recovering ahead of schedule.
Intervention at the source on Day {days} gave the {keystone} population enough runway to self-organise; the trophic cascade ran upward instead of down.
Field log closed Day {days}: {stressor} load suppressed to subcritical levels before secondary extinctions could propagate — a textbook early-strike result.
win_close: Health crossed 75% on Day {days} with the collapse timer nearly expired — the margin was thin, but the wetland is standing and that is the only number that matters.
The {keystone} population held through the final stress window; Ecosystem Health {health}% confirms a functional, if fragile, recovery — continued monitoring advised.
Day {days}: intervention arrived late enough that {stressor} scars remain visible in the sediment record, yet the food web closed intact — a narrow but legitimate restoration.
lose_timeout: The collapse timer expired at Health {health}% — {stressor} loading persisted long enough that cumulative trophic debt exceeded what the remaining {days} days could repay.
Time constraint, not intent, ended this run: {stressor} reduction was progressing but too slowly for the wetland's carrying-capacity decline curve.
Field log, final entry: the window closed at Day {days} with the meadow still degraded — the biology was tractable; the timeline was not.
lose_keystone: {keystone} reached local extinction on Day {days}; once the apex node collapses, the energy pyramid loses its regulatory ceiling and recovery without restocking is not mathematically viable.
The loss of {keystone} is a threshold event — prey release and trophic cascade inversion will restructure community composition faster than any remaining intervention can counteract.
Field note, Day {days}: {keystone} confirmed absent from survey transects; the wetland's functional integrity is broken at the top — this run is a data point, not a failure of effort.
lesson_root_cause: Suppressing {stressor} at its source gave downstream species the clean-water window they needed — treating the driver, not the symptom, is the lever with the longest reach.

Root-cause intervention against {stressor} allowed seagrass turbidity recovery to precede shrimp biomass gain, which then supported {keystone} prey density — the causal chain ran in the right direction.
The edge data showed {edgePct}% of stress flowing from {stressor}; allocating the first interventions there rather than at mid-trophic nodes was the decision that changed the trajectory.
lesson_symptom_only: Mid-trophic interventions slowed the decline but never addressed {stressor}; symptom management without source control is a rearguard action against arithmetic.
Resources spent stabilising consumer populations bought time but not recovery — {stressor} continued loading nutrients while the trophic buffer was being artificially maintained.
The graph confirms it: {edgePct}% of cascading stress originated at {stressor}, which went untreated; every intervention downstream was discounting a debt that kept compounding.
lesson_shallow_scan: Scan coverage was sufficient to identify surface-level species counts but the dependency edges between {keystone} and its prey guild were never resolved — keystone status remained invisible until it was too late.
Shallow survey confirmed presence/absence; it could not reveal that {stressor} was suppressing reproduction rather than adult survival, which changed the time-to-extinction estimate by roughly 40%.
The notebook filled with observations but the causal map stayed sparse — in degraded wetlands, what connects species matters as much as which species are present.
lesson_deep_scan: Full edge resolution on Day {days} exposed the {stressor}→seagrass→shrimp→{keystone} pathway before the cascade completed; the dependency map turned a reactive response into a targeted one.

Deep scan charges were expensive but the return was proportionate — knowing that {edgePct}% of network stress ran through a single bottleneck node compressed the intervention search space dramatically.
Mapping the hidden trophic links early converted ambiguous health readings into a ranked action list; field ecologists call this the difference between monitoring and understanding.
